tool_args.label: 2020 Bus Priority Improvements – Bronx Community Board 5

payload.title: 2020 Bus Priority Improvements
payload.description: Presentation to Bronx Community Board 5 Municipal Services Committee on December 3, 2019, covering 2020 bus priority improvements including corridors Tremont Avenue, University Avenue, Washington Bridge/181 St, and Fordham Road/207 St/University Heights Bridge.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: NYC DOT

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

tool_args.label: MTA
Bronx Community Board 5 Municipal Services Committee

payload.entity_name: Bronx Community Board 5 Municipal Services Committee

tool_args.label: Tremont Avenue

payload.description: Bus corridor carrying Bx36 (after redesign), 36,800 weekday riders. Bus speeds 4.3 mph average during PM peak. Key issues: congested corridor with slow bus speeds, buses caught in traffic, double parking, vehicles blocking bus stops.

tool_args.label: University Avenue

payload.description: Bus corridor serving Bx3, Bx36. Combined 55,000 weekday riders, 12,700 weekday riders on University Ave. Bus speeds 6.3 mph during AM Peak, 5.6 mph during PM Peak. Key issues: slow bus speeds and traffic congestion, buses frequently cannot pull to curb, double parking, long distances between pedestrian crossings.

tool_args.label: Washington Bridge / 181 St

payload.description: Bus corridor serving Bx3, Bx11, Bx13, Bx35, Bx36. Combined 111,400 weekday riders, 36,800 weekday riders cross the Washington Bridge. Bus speeds 9.9 mph during AM Peak, 8.2 mph during PM Peak. Key issues: slow bus speeds and traffic congestion, especially at intersections at ends of the bridge.

tool_args.label: Fordham Road / 207 St / University Heights Bridge

payload.description: Bus corridor serving Bx9, Bx12 SBS/Local, Bx17, Bx22, B-L 60, B-L 61, B-L 62. 113,700 weekday riders (NYCT routes only), 53,100 use the Bx12 SBS and Local. Bus speeds 6.8 mph during AM Peak, 5.9 during PM Peak. Key issues: significant and consistent decline in bus speeds in recent years, high curb demand, frequently blocked bus lanes, traffic and sidewalk congestion.

tool_args.label: Bx36

payload.description: Bus route on Tremont Avenue and University Avenue corridors. Carries 36,800 weekday riders on Tremont Ave segment.

tool_args.label: 2020 Bronx Bus Priority Corridors (CB5)

payload.description: NYC DOT analyzed 46 major Bronx corridors as part of MTA's Bronx Bus Network Redesign process. Selected 10 highest ranking corridors, including 4 within CB5: Tremont Avenue, University Avenue, Washington Bridge/181 St, Fordham Road/207 St/University Heights Bridge. Study of potential treatments including bus lanes, queue jump signals, bus boarding islands, short segments of bus/bike lanes, offset bus lanes.

tool_args.label: Webster Avenue Southbound Bus Lane Improvement

payload.location: Webster Avenue, E 176 St – E 174 St
payload.description: Addition of southbound bus lane resulted in 37% improvement in bus speeds in the PM Peak. Pedestrian safety enhancements calmed turns onto Cross Bronx Expwy. Pedestrian island to be installed in Spring 2020.

tool_args.label: Winter/Spring 2020 Next Steps

payload.description: Analyze traffic and bus data, develop draft street design plans, present draft plan to Community Board 5 and local stakeholders

tool_args.label: Later in 2020 Next Steps

payload.description: Develop detailed final plans, present detailed final plans to Community Board 5 and local stakeholders, implement project improvements

tool_args.label: Webster Avenue Pedestrian Island Installation

payload.description: Pedestrian island will be installed on Webster Avenue between E 176 St and E 174 St

tool_args.label: Tremont Ave b/n Morris Ave & Grand Concourse treatment study

payload.location_text: Tremont Ave between Morris Ave and Grand Concourse
payload.description: Potential treatment studying elimination of curbside parking lane to create dedicated bus stop area. Benefits: increased bus speeds by eliminating weaving in/out of traffic, improved bus stop accessibility. Potential treatment shows 10' travel lane and 13' bus stop replacing 14' shared lane and 9' parking lane.

normalized_location.raw_text: Tremont Ave between Morris Ave and Grand Concourse

tool_args.label: Tremont Ave b/n Anthony Ave and Valentine Ave treatment study

payload.location_text: Tremont Avenue between Anthony Ave and Valentine Ave
payload.description: Potential treatment showing new 11' bus lane replacing existing 11' shared lane. Benefits: increased bus speeds by eliminating weaving in/out of traffic.

normalized_location.raw_text: Tremont Avenue between Anthony Ave and Valentine Ave

tool_args.label: University Avenue south of Tremont Ave treatment study

payload.location_text: University Avenue, South of Tremont Ave
payload.description: Potential treatment: 10.5' bus lane, 8.5' boarding island. Benefits: increased bus speeds by eliminating weaving in/out of stops, improved bus stop accessibility, reduced crossing distances and improved pedestrian safety, enhanced bicycle safety and comfort, reduced sidewalk congestion.

normalized_location.raw_text: University Avenue, South of Tremont Ave

tool_args.label: University Avenue north of Tremont Ave treatment study

payload.location_text: University Avenue, North of Tremont Ave
payload.description: Potential treatment: 11' shared bike/bus lane, 9' boarding island. Benefits: streamlined boarding, increased bus speeds by eliminating weaving in/out of stops, improved bus stop accessibility, reduced vehicle/bicycle conflicts, reduced sidewalk congestion.

normalized_location.raw_text: University Avenue, North of Tremont Ave

tool_args.label: University Avenue and Fordham Rd queue jump signal

payload.description: Investigating queue jump signal at this intersection in both directions. Potential treatment shows 12' queue jump/right turn bay, 5' bike lane. Benefits: buses and bikes can bypass general traffic, increased bus speeds, reduced vehicle/bus/bike conflicts.

tool_args.label: Washington Bridge bus lanes proposal

payload.location_text: Washington Bridge, Amsterdam Ave to University Ave
payload.description: Proposed bus lanes on Washington Bridge. Potential treatment: converting two 10.5' travel lanes to 10.5' bus lanes (one curb lane in each direction). Benefits: increased bus speeds, direct decrease in travel times for 36,800 bus riders, improved reliability for 111,400 riders on all 5 MTA bus routes.

normalized_location.raw_text: Washington Bridge, Amsterdam Ave to University Ave

tool_args.label: Better Buses Action Plan goals

payload.claim_text: Mayor's 2019 State of the City: Improve bus speeds 25% by 2020. Better Buses Action Plan released April 2019. 24 priority projects announced for 2019 to improve bus service across all 5 boroughs.

tool_args.label: Bronx corridor selection methodology

payload.claim_text: As part of MTA's Bronx Bus Network Redesign process, NYC DOT analyzed 46 major Bronx corridors to identify where bus lanes and other treatments would speed up buses and allow the MTA to operate more frequent service. NYC DOT selected 10 of the highest ranking corridors, including 4 within CB5: Tremont Avenue, University Avenue, Washington Bridge / 181 St, Fordham Road / 207 St / University Heights Bridge.

tool_args.label: Fordham Road declining bus speeds

payload.claim_text: Significant and consistent decline in bus speeds in recent years on Fordham Road. High curb demand, frequently blocked bus lanes, traffic and sidewalk congestion.

tool_args.label: Washington Bridge bus lane benefits

payload.claim_text: Proposed bus lanes on Washington Bridge would provide direct decrease in travel times for 36,800 bus riders and improved reliability for 111,400 riders on all 5 MTA bus routes, as well as improved bus speeds for all other buses including school buses and non-MTA buses.
